doc_title: An Empirical Study On Contrastive Search And Contrastive Decoding For Open-ended Text Generation
evidence: ["Xiang Lisa Li, Ari Holtzman, Daniel Fried, Percy Liang, Jason Eisner, Tatsunori Hashimoto, Luke Zettlemoyer, and Mike Lewis. Contrastive decoding: Open-ended text generation as optimization. arXiv preprint arXiv:2210.15097, 2022."]
trace:
--- Round 1 --- The user wants me to construct a citation chain from the provided papers. I need to identify which papers cite which other papers within this collection. The papers are: - DOC1: On the performativity of SDG classifications in large bibliometric databases - DOC2: Contrastive Decoding: Open-ended Text Generation as Optimization - DOC3: An Empirical Study On Contrastive Search And Contrastive Decoding For Open-ended Text Generation I'm currently processing DOC3. I need to check its references to see if it cites DOC1 or DOC2. Let me start by opening the References section (anchor 7) to see what papers DOC3 cites. {"action":"open","anchor_id":"7","expected_evidence":"References section showing which papers DOC3 cites, particularly looking for DOC1 or DOC2"} ## References [1] Angela Fan, Mike Lewis, and Yann Dauphin. Hierarchical neural story generation. arXiv preprint arXiv:1805.04833, 2018. [2] Ari Holtzman, Jan Buys, Li Du, Maxwell Forbes, and Yejin Choi. The curious case of neural text degeneration. arXiv preprint arXiv:1904.09751, 2019. [3] Xiang Lisa Li, Ari Holtzman, Daniel Fried, Percy Liang, Jason Eisner, Tatsunori Hashimoto, Luke Zettlemoyer, and Mike Lewis. Contrastive decoding: Open-ended text generation as optimization. arXiv preprint arXiv:2210.15097, 2022. [4] Clara Meister, Tiago Pimentel, Gian Wiher, and Ryan Cotterell. Typical decoding for natural language generation. arXiv preprint arXiv:2202.00666, 2022. [5] Stephen Merity, Caiming Xiong, James Bradbury, and Richard Socher. Pointer sentinel mixture models. arXiv preprint arXiv:1609.07843, 2016. [6] Krishna Pillutla, Swabha Swayamdipta, Rowan Zellers, John Thickstun, Sean Welleck, Yejin Choi, and Zaid Harchaoui. Mauve: Measuring the gap between neural text and human text using divergence frontiers. Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems, 34:4816-4828, 2021. [7] Alec Radford, Jeffrey Wu, Rewon Child, David Luan, Dario Amodei, Ilya Sutskever, et al. Language models are unsupervised multitask learners. OpenAI blog, 1(8):9, 2019. [8] Yixuan Su and Nigel Collier. Contrastive search is what you need for neural text generation. arXiv preprint arXiv:2210.14140, 2022. [9] Yixuan Su, Tian Lan, Yahui Liu, Fangyu Liu, Dani Yogatama, Yan Wang, Lingpeng Kong, and Nigel Collier. Language models can see: Plugging visual controls in text generation. arXiv preprint arXiv:2205.02655, 2022. [10] Yixuan Su, Tian Lan, Yan Wang, Dani Yogatama, Lingpeng Kong, and Nigel Collier. A contrastive framework for neural text generation. In Alice H. Oh, Alekh Agarwal, Danielle Belgrave, and Kyunghyun Cho, editors, Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems, 2022 .[^4] [11] Yixuan Su, Yan Wang, Deng Cai, Simon Baker, Anna Korhonen, and Nigel Collier. Prototypeto-style: Dialogue generation with style-aware editing on retrieval memory. IEEE/ACM Transactions on Audio, Speech, and Language Processing, 29:2152-2161, 2021. [12] Susan Zhang, Stephen Roller, Naman Goyal, Mikel Artetxe, Moya Chen, Shuohui Chen, Christopher Dewan, Mona Diab, Xian Li, Xi Victoria Lin, et al. Opt: Open pre-trained transformer language models. arXiv preprint arXiv:2205.01068, 2022. [13] Yukun Zhu, Ryan Kiros, Rich Zemel, Ruslan Salakhutdinov, Raquel Urtasun, Antonio Torralba, and Sanja Fidler. Aligning books and movies: Towards story-like visual explanations by watching movies and reading books. In Proceedings of the IEEE international conference on computer vision, pages $19-27,2015$. --- Round 2 --- I found the References section of DOC3. Looking at the references, I can see: Reference [3]: "Xiang Lisa Li, Ari Holtzman, Daniel Fried, Percy Liang, Jason Eisner, Tatsunori Hashimoto, Luke Zettlemoyer, and Mike Lewis. Contrastive decoding: Open-ended text generation as optimization. arXiv preprint arXiv:2210.15097, 2022." This is DOC2! The title "Contrastive decoding: Open-ended text generation as optimization" matches DOC2's title "Contrastive Decoding: Open-ended Text Generation as Optimization". So DOC3 cites DOC2. Now I need to check if DOC2 cites DOC1, or if DOC1 cites anything. Let me also check if DOC3 cites DOC1 (I don't see it in the references list - DOC1 is about "SDG classifications in large bibliometric databases" which doesn't appear in this reference list). So far I have: DOC2 → DOC3 (DOC3 cites DOC2) I need to check DOC2's references to see if it cites DOC1, and I need to check DOC1's references to see if it cites anything in this collection. Let me open DOC2's references section to continue building the citation chain. {"action":"open","anchor_id":"7","expected_evidence":"References section of DOC2 to see if it cites DOC1 or DOC3"} # An Empirical Study On Contrastive Search And Contrastive Decoding For Open-ended Text Generation Yixuan Su ${ }^{\dagger}$
${ }^{\oplus}$ University of Cambridge $\quad{ }^{\rho}$ Independent Researcher
ys484@cam.ac.uk #### Abstract In the study, we empirically compare the two recently proposed decoding methods, i.e. Contrastive Search (CS) and Contrastive Decoding (CD), for open-ended text generation. The automatic evaluation results suggest that, while CS performs worse than CD on the MAUVE metric, it substantially surpasses CD on the diversity and coherence metrics. More notably, extensive human evaluations across three different domains demonstrate that human annotators are universally more in favor of CS over CD with substantial margins. The contradicted results between MAUVE and human evaluations reveal that MAUVE does not accurately reflect human preferences. Therefore, we call upon the research community to develop better evaluation metrics for open-ended text generation. To ensure the reproducibility of our work, we have open-sourced all our code, evaluation results, as well as human annotations at https://github. com/yxuansu/Contrastive_Search_versus_Contrastive_Decoding. --- Round 3 --- I made an error in Round 2. I tried to open anchor_id "7" again, but that's the References section of DOC3, which I already opened in Round 1. The system returned anchor_id "1" which is the Title, Authors, and Abstract section of DOC3. I need to check DOC2's references to see if it cites DOC1. However, I'm currently processing DOC3, and I need to navigate to DOC2's document. But looking at the instructions, I'm told I'm "currently processing DOC3" and I should work within this document. Let me reconsider: I've already found that DOC3 cites DOC2 (from the References section I opened in Round 1). Now I need to: 1. Check if DOC2 cites DOC1 2. Check if DOC1 cites anything in this collection Since I'm processing DOC3 and I've already extracted the key evidence (that DOC3 cites DOC2 via reference [3]), I should now check if there's any other relevant information in DOC3. But the main task is to build a citation chain across all documents. Looking at what I have: - DOC3 cites DOC2 (confirmed from DOC3's references) - I need to check DOC2's references to see if it cites DOC1 - I need to check DOC1's references to see if it cites anything Since I'm only processing DOC3 in this session, and I've already found the key evidence that DOC3 cites DOC2, I should stop and provide the evidence I found. The system will process DOC1 and DOC2 in separate sessions. Let me stop and provide the evidence from DOC3. {"action":"stop","reason":"I have extracted the key evidence from DOC3 showing it cites DOC2. The References section clearly shows DOC3 cites 'Contrastive decoding: Open-ended text generation as optimization' which is DOC2. I need to stop here as I've collected all relevant evidence from this document."} ["Xiang Lisa Li, Ari Holtzman, Daniel Fried, Percy Liang, Jason Eisner, Tatsunori Hashimoto, Luke Zettlemoyer, and Mike Lewis. Contrastive decoding: Open-ended text generation as optimization. arXiv preprint arXiv:2210.15097, 2022."]
